steps.title: Find the quadratic's minimum
steps.content:
Type the function into Desmos as written:
$$p(x)=2x^{2}+20x+15$$

Graph it or use the vertex formula to find the minimum value. The requested value is $-35$.

steps.title: Find the vertex x-coordinate
steps.content: For any quadratic $ax^2 + bx + c$, the vertex (minimum when $a \gt  0$) is at $x = -\frac{b}{2a}$. For $p(x) = 2x^2 + 20x + 15$: $$x = -\frac{20}{2(2)} = -\frac{20}{4} = -5.$$

steps.title: Evaluate p(−5) to get the minimum
steps.content: $$p(-5) = 2(25) + 20(-5) + 15 = 50 - 100 + 15 = -35.$$ Since $a = 2 \gt 0$ the parabola opens upward, confirming this is a minimum. That matches Choice A — -35.
